title: ChatGPT Is Getting Its Biggest Upgrade Ever
summary: OpenAI is reported to be transforming ChatGPT from a chatbot into a comprehensive AI super app integrating Codex, agents, image generation, third-party applications, and workplace automation. The expansion includes new security features like Lockdown Mode to protect against prompt injection attacks and a collaboration with Broadcom on custom AI accelerators.
writeup: OpenAI is overhauling ChatGPT into a multi-modal AI super app anchored by Codex, its fast-growing coding and automation agent, aiming to turn the product from a chatbot into a persistent personal and enterprise agent that handles tasks end-to-end. The strategic shift is driven by competition from Anthropic's Claude Code, which internal benchmarks placed ahead of OpenAI in programming ability in autumn 2024, and by investor pressure to convert a 900-million-plus consumer user base into serious recurring revenue. The through-line is that code, not browser automation, is the more effective substrate for AI to complete computer work, making Codex the foundation for a general agent layer rather than a niche developer tool.
key_points: Codex's user base grew sixfold in under two months after its desktop app launch, weekly active users surpassed 5 million by end of May, Sam Altman reported 5% daily usage growth, and Greg Brockman reported 50% week-over-week growth in enterprise Codex revenue.

OpenAI merged the ChatGPT, Codex, and API teams into a single core product and platform department under Tibo Saty in May, and simultaneously shelved the ChatGPT shopping checkout feature and reportedly shut down Sora, signaling a deliberate resource shift toward coding agents and enterprise tools.

GPT-5.5, released in April, is substantially better at long-term multi-step tasks than its predecessors, which is what gave Codex enough reliability for developers to hand off a complete plan and trust the agent to execute it without step-by-step supervision.

OpenAI deprioritized its Operator browser-automation agent after concluding that writing code is a more effective interface for complex computer tasks than having AI imitate human mouse clicks on websites.

Anthropic's annualized revenue reportedly reached $4.7 billion by May, driven by Claude Code and enterprise adoption, while OpenAI's annualized revenue surpassed $3 billion; both labs are converging on IPO-style revenue expectations and competing directly for enterprise developer spend.

Around 2 million businesses use OpenAI products and account for roughly 40% of its revenue, a share the company is targeting to raise to 50% by end of year, while more than 900 million consumer users are mostly on free tiers, making the enterprise coding-agent market the critical monetization lever.
